text.title: OpenAI Batch API for Bulk Embeddings and Completions
text.summary: 50% cost reduction batch workflow: JSONL file creation, submission, status polling, and result ordering via custom_id.

rule.statement: Assign meaningful custom_id values (e.g., doc_{id}_chunk_{n}) to every batch request; always use custom_id to reconstruct result order — never assume FIFO output ordering.
rule.rationale: The Batch API returns results in arbitrary order. Agents that rely on positional ordering will silently misattribute results. custom_id is the only reliable correlation key.

rule.statement: Use the Batch API for nightly re-indexing and offline enrichment only; synchronous embedding is correct for interactive query paths — the 24-hour completion window makes Batch unsuitable for real-time.
rule.rationale: Batch API has a 24-hour completion window and a 50K requests/batch ceiling. Interactive queries cannot wait 24 hours; nightly jobs benefit from the 50% cost reduction.

example.code:
import json from openai import OpenAI
client = OpenAI()
# Build JSONL batch file requests = [ { "custom_id": f"doc_{doc_id}_chunk_{chunk_n}", "method": "POST", "url": "/v1/embeddings", "body": { "model": "text-embedding-3-small", "input": chunk_text, }, } for doc_id, chunk_n, chunk_text in chunks ]
with open("batch.jsonl", "w") as f: for req in requests: f.write(json.dumps(req) + "\n")
# Submit batch_file = client.files.create(file=open("batch.jsonl", "rb"), purpose="batch") batch = client.batches.create( input_file_id=batch_file.id, endpoint="/v1/embeddings", completion_window="24h", )
# Poll until complete, then parse by custom_id batch = client.batches.retrieve(batch.id) if batch.status == "completed": lines = client.files.content(batch.output_file_id).text.strip().split("\n") results = {json.loads(l)["custom_id"]: json.loads(l) for l in lines}

antipattern.description: Embedding sensitive PII (names, emails, IDs) into a shared vector store.
antipattern.reason: Data leaks via nearest-neighbor queries are a real attack surface — an adversary can extract PII by querying with known PII fragments and retrieving neighbors.
